picks.game: Philadelphia 76ers @ Boston Celtics
picks.rationale: The Boston Celtics are in dominant form, boasting an 8-2 record in their last 10 games with a +13.7 point differential, including a current 3-game winning streak. They decisively defeated the 76ers 123-91 in Game 1 of the playoffs. Crucially, Philadelphia will be without star center Joel Embiid, who is out due to post-appendectomy surgery recovery, significantly impacting their offensive and defensive capabilities. The Celtics also hold a strong historical advantage, winning 83 of 132 games against the 76ers since 2004.

picks.game: Portland Trail Blazers @ San Antonio Spurs
picks.rationale: The San Antonio Spurs enter this Game 2 matchup in excellent form, with an 8-2 record in their last 10 games and a robust +13.6 point differential. They comfortably won Game 1 against the Trail Blazers, 111-98. The Trail Blazers will be severely hampered by the absence of their star player, Damian Lillard, who is out due to left Achilles tendon injury management. The Spurs also hold a strong head-to-head advantage, winning 4 of their last 5 matchups against Portland.

picks.game: Houston Rockets @ Los Angeles Lakers
picks.rationale: The Los Angeles Lakers are playing at home and have a strong recent record there, going 9-1 in their last 10 home games while averaging 117.8 points per game. They also hold a significant head-to-head advantage over the Rockets, winning 4 of their last 5 encounters, including a 107-98 victory in Game 1. The Houston Rockets are dealing with key injuries, with Fred VanVleet out for the season and star player Kevin Durant listed as questionable with a right knee contusion, which could severely impact their offensive output. Despite the Lakers missing Doncic and Reaves, LeBron James' playoff performance remains a critical factor.

picks.game: Philadelphia 76ers @ Boston Celtics
picks.rationale: With Joel Embiid, the 76ers' primary scoring threat, officially ruled OUT due to post-appendectomy surgery recovery, Philadelphia's offensive production is expected to significantly decrease. While the Celtics scored 123 points in Game 1, the 76ers only managed 91 points, resulting in a total of 214, which is below the current line of 216.5. The Celtics' strong defense, allowing only 107.3 points per game in their last 10 outings, further supports an under trend. Without Embiid, the 76ers will likely struggle to contribute enough offense to push the total over.

picks.game: Portland Trail Blazers @ San Antonio Spurs
picks.rationale: Both the San Antonio Spurs and Portland Trail Blazers have demonstrated high-scoring capabilities in their recent games. The Spurs have averaged 124.2 points per game in their last 10 contests, while the Trail Blazers have averaged 116.3 points per game over the same period. Although Game 1 finished with 209 points, the absence of Damian Lillard for the Trail Blazers might lead to a more free-flowing, less structured offensive game, potentially increasing scoring opportunities on both ends. The Spurs have also shown a tendency for higher-scoring games, with their last 10 games averaging a combined 234.8 points.

picks.game: Montreal Canadiens vs Tampa Bay Lightning
picks.rationale: The Montreal Canadiens secured an overtime victory in Game 1, demonstrating their ability to compete closely with the Lightning. Tampa Bay enters this game in questionable form, having lost four of their last five games both straight up and against the spread. Despite some injuries for Montreal, the line movement has shown a slight shift in their favor, suggesting bettors see value in them keeping the game tight. This pick relies on Montreal maintaining their competitive edge and either winning or losing by a single goal.

picks.game: Boston Bruins vs Buffalo Sabres
picks.rationale: The Buffalo Sabres clinched the top seed in the Atlantic Division, highlighting their strong performance throughout the regular season and earning them home-ice advantage in this playoff series. While the Boston Bruins have a historical edge in head-to-head matchups, Buffalo's current status as a division leader and their home-ice advantage in Game 2 are significant factors. The Sabres will be highly motivated to capitalize on their strong season and protect their home turf.

picks.game: Utah Mammoth vs Vegas Golden Knights
picks.rationale: The Vegas Golden Knights decisively won Game 1 against the Utah Mammoth with a 4-2 score and displayed strong underlying metrics, boasting an expected goals differential of +1.19. Vegas has been in excellent form leading into the playoffs, ranking second in the league in expected goal differential since the Olympic break. Despite the absence of William Karlsson, the Golden Knights' overall team strength, home-ice advantage, and dominant Game 1 performance make the -1.5 spread at plus odds a compelling high-confidence pick.

picks.game: Los Angeles Kings vs Colorado Avalanche
picks.rationale: The Colorado Avalanche are a dominant force with a 55-16-11 record and enter this game with no reported injuries. In stark contrast, the Los Angeles Kings are severely depleted by multiple key injuries, including Kevin Fiala being out for the season, and Andrei Kuzmenko, Jeff Malott, and Alex Turcotte all sidelined. Colorado has a commanding head-to-head record against the Kings, winning their last five encounters, including a 4-2 victory in Game 1 of this series. The Avalanche's superior health, recent form, and historical dominance strongly suggest another multi-goal victory.

picks.game: Montreal Canadiens vs Tampa Bay Lightning
picks.rationale: Game 1 of this series between the Canadiens and Lightning was a high-scoring affair, finishing 4-3 for a total of 7 goals, easily surpassing the current 5.5 line. The Tampa Bay Lightning have also shown a recent trend towards higher-scoring games, with the Over hitting in four of their last five contests. Both teams have demonstrated offensive capabilities, with Montreal's Juraj Slafkovský recording a hat trick and Tampa Bay's Brandon Hagel scoring two goals in Game 1. These factors, combined with potential defensive adjustments, point to another game exceeding the total.

picks.game: Houston Astros @ Cleveland Guardians
picks.rationale: The Houston Astros offense has been performing well recently, scoring four or more runs in their last four games, including nine runs in the series opener against the Guardians. While Cleveland has a promising young starter, the Astros have shown they can hit. Historically, the head-to-head matchups between these two teams have seen the Over hit in 66.7% of games. Furthermore, the total line has seen movement upwards from 7.5 to 8, and now to 8.5, indicating public and sharp money leaning towards a higher-scoring affair.

picks.game: Atlanta Braves @ Washington Nationals
picks.rationale: The Atlanta Braves are in excellent form, boasting a 16-7 record and riding a six-game winning streak. Their offense is one of the best in MLB, with a .274 team batting average and a .446 slugging percentage. The Braves have also dominated recent head-to-head matchups against the Nationals, winning four of the last five games, including a 9-4 victory on April 20, 2026. Despite some Braves injuries, their overall team strength, superior pitching staff (2.68 ERA vs. Nationals' 5.71 ERA), and positive line movement towards Atlanta make this a high-confidence pick.

picks.game: New York Yankees @ Boston Red Sox
picks.rationale: The New York Yankees have a strong historical and recent advantage over the Boston Red Sox, winning four of their last five head-to-head contests, including a 4-0 shutout on October 2, 2025. The Red Sox are currently dealing with significant pitching injuries, with Tanner Houck on the 60-day IL and Sonny Gray's status uncertain after a hamstring injury. The Yankees are also in better recent form, holding a 4-1 record in their last five games. These factors, combined with the favorable moneyline odds, suggest a strong betting opportunity for the Yankees.

picks.game: San Diego Padres @ Colorado Rockies
picks.rationale: The San Diego Padres have been utterly dominant in their head-to-head matchups against the Colorado Rockies this season, winning all five games and covering the -1.5 spread in each instance. Padres starter Randy Vasquez has been effective, with his team covering the spread in all four of his starts this season. While Coors Field is known for high-scoring games, the Padres' deeper lineup and stronger bullpen give them a significant advantage. The consistent H2H performance and Vasquez's reliability make the Padres -1.5 a high-confidence pick.

picks.game: Los Angeles Dodgers @ San Francisco Giants
picks.rationale: The Los Angeles Dodgers possess a powerful offense, currently batting .293 as a team with a .507 slugging percentage. While Dodgers' starter Yoshinobu Yamamoto has been excellent this season, Giants' starter Landen Roupp has a career 7.30 ERA against the Dodgers, indicating potential for runs. The total line has seen upward movement from 7 to 7.5, suggesting expectations for a higher-scoring game. Given the Dodgers' offensive prowess and Roupp's past struggles against them, combined with the rivalry atmosphere, the Over 7.5 is a strong play.

picks.game: Charlotte FC @ Orlando City SC
picks.rationale: Orlando City SC is in very poor form, currently sitting 30th in the MLS table with a -18 goal differential, indicating significant defensive struggles. A remarkable 86% of Orlando City's games this season have gone Over 2.5 goals, demonstrating a consistent trend of high-scoring matches. The previous head-to-head encounter between these two teams resulted in a 2-2 draw, further supporting the likelihood of goals. Charlotte FC also recently contributed to a higher-scoring game with a 2-1 victory over New York City FC.

picks.game: LA Galaxy @ Columbus Crew SC
picks.rationale: LA Galaxy has shown a strong tendency for high-scoring games, with 75% of their matches featuring both teams scoring and an average of 3.38 goals per game this season. Their recent results include a 2-2 draw against FC Dallas and a 4-2 win in the CONCACAF Champions Cup, highlighting their offensive capabilities. Columbus Crew SC has also been involved in goal-filled contests, with recent scores like a 3-1 win over Atlanta United and a 1-1 draw with Orlando City. Given the offensive output and defensive vulnerabilities of both sides, the +130 odds for Over 3.5 goals offer excellent value.

picks.game: D.C. United @ New York Red Bulls
picks.rationale: D.C. United consistently plays low-scoring matches, with only 17% of their games seeing both teams score and an average of just 2.00 total goals per game this season. Their last league match resulted in a 0-0 draw against Philadelphia Union, underscoring their defensive focus and offensive struggles. With D.C. United's propensity for tight, low-scoring affairs and the favorable +100 odds, betting the Under 2.5 presents a strong opportunity. This pick aligns with D.C. United's overall form, which places them 20th in the MLS.

picks.game: FC Cincinnati @ New York City FC
picks.rationale: Both FC Cincinnati and New York City FC have demonstrated a strong inclination towards high-scoring games this season. FC Cincinnati's matches average an impressive 4.00 goals, with 88% of their games seeing both teams score. Their most recent outing was a thrilling 3-3 draw against Chicago Fire. New York City FC also boasts a high BTTS percentage of 84% and averages 2.97 goals per game. These combined offensive trends and defensive lapses make the Over 2.5 a statistically robust and high-confidence pick.

picks.game: New England Revolution @ Atlanta United FC
picks.rationale: Atlanta United FC has severely struggled offensively this season, scoring only 6 goals in 8 games and maintaining a 0% BTTS rate, with their matches averaging just 1.34 goals. This anemic attack, coupled with New England Revolution's very poor away form (0 wins, 0 draws, 3 losses), points towards a low-scoring encounter. While New England has a better overall record, their road struggles in scoring will likely be a factor. The -110 odds for Under 2.5 are attractive, reflecting Atlanta's consistent inability to find the back of the net.
